title: How to Avoid a Climate Disaster

chapters.title: Cutting greenhouse emissions to zero - an impossible task?
chapters.text:
Currently, 51 billion tonnes of greenhouse gases are released every single year. We need to get that down to as close to zero as possible. A hard task for sure, but one which shouldn’t be ignored.

The more troubling news is that these gases are increasing in volume rather than decreasing. However, if we manage to achieve this huge task, we can avoid the very worst and potentially catastrophic effects of climate change. Stay as we are and we’re on course for a huge disaster.

Reducing billions down to zero is going to take serious action. Failure to put in the effort could mean the Earth becomes too hot for human survival.

To reach this huge aim, every country needs to pull together and we need to use the initiative we have, along with newly developed ones. Without this, the world is just going to keep getting hotter.

In 2020 and early 2021, there has been a slight decrease in the amount of emitted gases, due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Everything ground to a halt, yet the effect on the climate was minimal. This just goes to show that a huge effort is needed to avoid major disaster in the future.

It’s not a pass-fail exam where everything’s great if we get a 100 percent reduction and everything’s a disaster if we get only a 99 percent reduction. But the bigger the reduction, the bigger the benefit. ~ Bill Gates

chapters.title: The connection between greenhouse emissions and everyday actions
chapters.text:
Reducing emissions becomes harder still when you start to look at exactly what causes gases to be released into the atmosphere — almost everything! From the plastic brush you use to detangle your hair in the morning to the car you drive, the bread you ate for toast at breakfast to the machine you use to cut the grass in your garden. Almost everything either releases gases or produces gases at the production stage.

The struggle is real — many of your daily actions rely upon equipment which emits greenhouses gases, either when used, or when they were made.

Another damaging material that seems to be almost everywhere is oil. It’s not possible to simply stop using oil; the world uses billions of gallons daily and there is currently no solid and reliable material that can be used to replace it. It’s far easier to simply carry on the way we’re going, but the consequences of that are unthinkable.

Of course, greenhouse gas emitting materials are used because they’re cheap and easy to produce. Most things which are good for the planet cost a little more and that’s the sticking point of the whole issue.

Fossil fuels, including coal and petroleum, are cheap to produce. Finding a cost effective replacement has proved to be difficult.

We don’t have the time to hang around and wait for decades to make changes, and throughout history it’s been shown that beneficial changes take a lot of time. Put simply, we need to put our thinking caps on and take this crisis more seriously.

Did you know? Carbon dioxide is the most harmful of all the greenhouse gases at it’s currently at a level higher than ever before.

chapters.title: The mission to reach clean electricity status
chapters.text:
Electricity is something we take for granted. We simply flick a switch and our phone charges, our laptops work, our TV lights up for action, and we can see around the room during darkness hours. The fact that we rely heavily upon electricity is even more troublesome when you realize that electricity emits a huge amount of greenhouse gases.

Electricity is a huge issue for global warming, but it’s also the one thing we rely on. Carbon-free electricity will be a worthwhile mission.

One option to change this is to look towards developing zero-carbon electricity. By doing this, we’re taking a huge step in the right direction and this will also help us to cut out the carbon in other areas. The other problem is that not everyone has access to enough electricity as it stands currently, especially in Africa.

Environmentally friendly types of power — such as hydropower — tried in the past worked to a degree, but not enough. Solar power is also ineffective 24/7 because the sun doesn’t shine all the time. A potential fix for this problem is to store a certain amount of the electricity yielded throughout the day and use that during the evening.

Storing solar energy could be a solution to the world’s huge electricity problem.

The other issue is that by trying to decarbonize electricity, the price is bound to increase and that will be passed on to the consumer. If we were to store solar power and use it during the evening hours, it would depend on the size of the battery, the type, and how long they last for as to how much of an increase the consumer would need to pay.

chapters.title: Cutting production emissions would give us a huge head start
chapters.text:
Apart from electricity, production and manufacturing are areas which create the most greenhouse gas. We face a huge issue here, because there are no currently sustainable ways to produce materials without emitting gases. For example, concrete is used for logistics, and is vital for movement and transportation, but how do we create concrete in a non-emitting way?

The world needs glass, concrete, cement and steel to make the items we need for our lives. These materials have been life-changing, but they’re heavy on gas production.

Most of the materials we rely on emit greenhouse gases. Capturing gases at the point of production is a way to solve this issue.

You’ve no doubt heard about plastics. We know that these stick around for a long time, and that once they’re thrown away, they end up sitting in landfill sites and even find their way into the seas and oceans around the world, often for hundreds and even thousands of years.

A possible solution is to produce materials and try to catch the carbon emissions with a device. By doing that, we’re still creating the materials we need, but we’re drastically cutting emissions, without any interruption to our daily lives.

We need to focus on changing mind-sets towards recycling. This is a way to reduce production and damage.

The issue of plastic is slightly easier to solve. We need to focus more on recycling, but the current plastic that is sitting in landfills and our oceans is something we need to solve. By cleaning the oceans, we can promote the good health of marine-life and ecological systems. Also, by being more recycling savvy as a world population, we can ensure that the same issue never repeats itself again. In many ways, it’s a mind-set change, but it’s one we all need to adopt.

Did you know? Around 8 million tons of plastic are thought to end up in the world’s oceans every single year.

chapters.title: The planet takes a big hit from farming and transportation
chapters.text:
As if electricity and materials aren’t big enough problems on their own, farming and transportation are also major issues. The way we grow our food creates a certain amount of greenhouse gas. Thankfully, this is a slightly easier problem to solve.

We raise animals for food, but the animals themselves produce gases. This is mostly through digestion and the production of methane. Whilst carbon dioxide is the worst type of greenhouse gas in terms of prevalence, methane is a huge issue too.

If we want to get near net-zero emissions, we’re going to have to figure out how to grow plants and raise animals while reducing and eventually eliminating greenhouse gases. ~ Bill Gates

The foods which animals eat often create gas in their stomachs, as a result of fermentation. Then, when they release this gas naturally or open their bowels, methane, sulfur, and ammonia, are released into the atmosphere. Efforts to create animal feed which eventually produces less methane is a good go-to here, but we also need to make sure that we teach poorer farmers how to handle manure in a way which reduces the gaseous effect on the environment.

Animal feed, which minimizes gas production and fermentation in the stomach, is a good way to reduce the release of methane into the atmosphere.

It’s not just animals but fertilizers, too. We use these to help make food last longer but they are damaging for the environment. A way around this issue is to look toward coatings which are derived from plants. These coatings will help to give fruits and vegetables a longer shelf-life, without affecting how they taste.

Transportation of supplies and foods around the world also creates a problem in itself. Petroleum is a very cheap material, but it’s a huge problem in terms of emissions. Electrical cars could help out in a big way, but the price of purchasing these cars needs to come down in order to encourage consumers to buy them over regular gas-guzzlers.

Electric cars could go a long way to helping the environment, but they’re currently expensive to buy.

chapters.title: It’s up to us to change things
chapters.text:
We can debate different technologies that can be used to reduce greenhouse gases, but in the end, it comes down to government initiatives and the things that we do ourselves. Of course, governments have the power to create a huge difference but we shouldn’t assume that you and me can’t do anything at all.

It’s important to educate yourself on climate change and try to speak to friends and family members, to give them the knowledge they need too. The more people who are aware of the problem, the more we can do to alter the current course. In addition, you may be able to switch to an electric car rather than a petroleum car, and become more recycling-savvy in your home and workplace.

You shouldn’t assume that as one individual, you can’t make a difference. We can all drive change with small actions that build up over time.

If you’re able to, why not call your local government office and try to push the climate agenda further towards their attention? Educate yourself on the political figures who care about the planet and vote for them. Your vote matters, especially when it comes to changing the course of the planet.

You probably go shopping to some degree every day, so become more aware of what you’re buying and how much you’re wasting. Look at your home and how much heat you’re using versus how much you really need. Cut down on your energy bills and maybe go vegan!

These are all things we can do to make a difference to the future of the planet that we call home. The more people who make these changes, the more effective a movement it will become.

Vote for the right people, cut down on your energy bills, change your shopping habits. Make a planetary change!

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
Climate change is a subject which has long been on the agenda but always pushed to the bottom of the list. Over the course of 2020 and 2021, our heads were turned away from the planet and towards battling COVID-19. However, we can’t let our attention slip away from the huge disaster that is looming on the horizon if we don’t make changes quickly and effectively.

Cutting emissions down to zero is a huge task, but it’s not impossible. It will take effort from every single person who calls Earth their home, and this collective effort will only be enough if policies are put into place by Governments to drive change. The way in which we obtain our electricity needs to change, how we produce the food we eat and the materials that help us to build our roads, bridges, and houses too. Put simply, almost everything needs to be tweaked, but if we can do it, our lives will be better and our futures will be safer.

Every single person can do their bit. Look at your daily habits, look at what you eat, and look at how you run your home. Recycle and make as many tweaks as you can, whilst spreading the importance of this message far and wide. The future of the Earth really does rely upon every single one of us.

Try this:

1. If you haven’t already, start recycling in your home and at work. Have separate bins for plastics and other materials and take them to your local drop off point on a regular basis.

2. How much do you drive? Cut down on the amount you drive by half and notice how much fitter and healthier you feel, whilst doing your bit for climate change.

3. Do a little research into local political parties. Which ones have a climate change focus and which skim over the subject? Voting for the right people will encourage Governments to make positive environmental legislation changes.

Read it till the end?
